I would return for the food but would have liked better service.

Comments: I think he said his name was Zaid
If I could tick every box I would!

I've never arranged a work event and had a member of staff so attentive to us. Nothing was too much trouble, and once he was made aware of someone with allergies he went over and above to explain all of our available options and made some great recommendations. He was like a whirlwind, rushing around to get us settled and made us feel so welcome. His energy is 1000000%.  I was updated on when drinks would be arriving, and food would be served all throughout the night. We were there from 18.30 - 11.30 and not once did we need to ask twice for anything. He is a real credit to your team and company.

Paul on the door was fantastic, we had a very brief chat while I was getting some air, and not once did he take his eye off the ball engaging with customers coming and going, while holding our conversation. Another great member of your team. Also, Lauren was amazing with all the booking process, so helpful and quick. There were 14 of us in total so we booked 2 oches and every single time we've been the oches have been able to mix all players across the games so that as a team everyone can be mixed - this is absolutely critical when making a corporate booking. On arrival, we had the two oches but unfortunately we were told that the games/teams could not be mixed - the woman that we asked initially was really rude and dismissive, said she'd go and ask someone but as i walked back to the desk she didnt bother, which I suspected. I then asked another team member who said it's not possible, it's never been able to do that and accused both myself and the MD of lying. We carried on with playing the 2 oches seperately as we had no choice but i would not return with a corporate team again or with more than the 12 for a single oche. Visit Summary: Overall, the visit to Flight Club Bristol was fun and entertaining. The most enjoyable aspect of the visit was the Oche, with various interactive games that offered an excellent social experience. The venue, especially the gaming area, was well-maintained, clean and in good working order. The team was working together to create a relaxing and pleasant atmosphere. All the staff we encountered were approachable, friendly and attentive. The food and drinks were value for money, flavoursome and served in good portions for the price charged. There were a few suggestions for consideration. The gallic pizza bread could be prepared with more care to avoid a partly burnt base. All hand soap and lotion dispensers in the female toilet could be working properly. Based on the menu descriptions, the Padrón peppers were part of The Social Sharer but were served with The Signature Social that we ordered, which was a nice addition.

Comments: The Signature Platter was served hot on a clean serving board. The prawn crackers were crispy. The mixed olives were served in a small ramekin. The olives tasted savoury and tangy. The garlic pizza bread was cheesy and garlicky, but the base of the pizza bread was partly burnt and tasted bitter. The popcorn chicken and chorizo were glazed with honey, tasted savoury and sweet. The salt and pepper squid was served in a small cup. The squid was crispy on the outside and chewy on the inside. The pork belly bites were served in a bowl, tender and flavourful. The Padrón peppers were appetising and nicely seasoned with sea salt. The Peri-peri Burger and Smash Burger were served hot on clean plates with slaw and fries. The peri-peri chicken was flavoursome and crispy. The beef patties of the Smash Burger were juicy and tender. The burger buns were soft and fresh tasting. The slaw was crunchy and creamy. The fries were golden and crispy. The Meat Feast Pizza, Tandoori Pizza and Truffle Pizza were served hot on clean serving boards. All the pizzas were nicely sliced and the ingredients spread evenly across the pizzas. The pizzas tasted as anticipated based on the menu descriptions. The Meat Feast Pizza tasted savoury from peperoni, bacon and spiced beef. The Tandoori Pizza was drizzled with yoghurt which was minty and poppadoms which were crispy. The Truffle Pizza tasted of truffle and Parmesan. The Dessert Duo was served warm on a clean plate with 4 brownies and 4 beignets filled with caramel sauce. The beignets were sweet, tasted of caramel, soft and chewy. The brownies were dense, moist and sweet. I did not score 5/5 as the base of the garlic pizza bread was partly burnt and could be prepared with more care.

Overall, we had an enjoyable experience and would happily return. The highlight of the visit was the games which were fun, engaging, and easy to get into.

The food that we ordered was served to us extremely promptly and, in terms of quality, was definitely comparable to similar food outlets. 

The only recommendation that we would make would be for all the dart game options to be explained in detail, with the easier games for beginners to be pointed out, and the more complex games being recommended to experienced players.

Comments: Bar and waiting staff were fantastic. Had a very unpleasant encounter with the person on the door who resorted to a personal insult.

We were a party of 7 who were there to celebrate our twins sons’ 18th birthday. Out daughter would be joining us a little later and which I was told was absolutely fine. However on arrival my daughter was refused entry by the doorman as she didn’t have ID (she’s 21). I met her at the door and showed him a photo of her passport which I have on my phone. I explained that it was a family celebration but he refused to budge, saying that anyone who appears under 25 is asked for ID. When I asked why no one in our party was asked (there were 5 18-year olds) his response was to look me up and down and ask why he would need to ask me! I have absolutely no issue with rules as long as they are applied fairly and consistently. We witnessed other people who were obviously under 25 walking in without being asked for ID. I am unsure why my daughter was singled out and I was subjected to a personal insult about my appearance from a quite frankly arrogant, rude and deeply unpleasant individual. We have another family celebration soon and intended to return to Flight Club. This will probably not be the case now as I really would not like to encounter this person again. I would like to emphasise though how fantastic the rest of the Flight Club ream were. Such a shame our boys’ 18th birthday was spoiled by them being prevented from celebrating with their sister. Just for the record, the rest of our group all had valid photo  ID had they been asked for it.

Answer: I'm not sure what that is

Visit Summary: Flight club has great kerb appeal. It stands out on the High Street as it’s nicely decorated and kept clean and tidy. It was a little crowded as you walk into the entranceway, and we had to wait a few minutes before we were able to speak to the host, as there was a large group ahead of us who were being dealt with. I waited a few minutes until that group had been seen and then approached the welcome desk. The staff member was friendly, and I explained we had a booking. They checked my name off against the system and then asked me to follow him to the oche, where we were greeted by another staff member. They showed us how to set up the system and where to stow our coats. Another staff member arrived and asked if we were ready to order drinks and food. It took awhile to order the food due to the allergen issue. When it did arrive, the food was delicious. There was a nice ambience to the venue. There were large groups around us, and they all seemed to be having a lovely time, including our group. Definitely something I would return to do again. We found the whole experience thoroughly enjoyable.

Comments: We were here for a family birthday - one of our group had been previously visited and enjoyed the experience. It was busy when we arrived for a pre-booked slot, but even so we didn't anticipate a  45-minute wait for our drinks (by which time we were well into our games and food). The food actually arrived quicker than expected, but unfortunately two plates of food were almost cold. We alerted the man on the till to this and told him we wouldn't eat or pay for them. He wasn't the most responsive of employees, however he did say he would arranged for the two items to be replaced. This didn't happen.We eventually complained again to our original server - and also reminded her that our drinks still hadn't arrived. We were assured we wouldn't be charged for the uneaten cold items which were still sitting on our table. By now, the rest of the food had been consumed. Our server assured us we wouldn't be charged for the untouched cold food and would receive a 'complimentary' dish free of charge. Our drinks finally arrived - two were alcoholic although non-alcoholic versions had been ordered so they had to be replaced. Eventually, long after everything else had been consumed, a hot version of the cold food was brought to us. The lighting around the cash desk was very dim but our server assured us that she'd removed the cold items from our total bill. She HAD done this in fact, but it wasn't until we were back home that we realised that we had been charged in full for the 'complementary' replacement item. It's a very long time since we've experienced service quite this bad. If it wasn't for the fun element of the darts, I'd score a one-star review.

Flight Club in Cardiff is a very interesting place and definitely stands out for its atmosphere and decoration. The interior is beautifully designed. It has a classic English house feeling, with a lot of wooden elements, but there are also some touches that reminded me a bit of Art Nouveau, which gives the place a unique character.One detail I particularly liked was the staircase area, where there are many clocks decorating the walls. It’s a small thing but it really adds personality to the space. The venue also has a rooftop terrace, which is very nicely arranged with plants and heaters, so it’s comfortable even when the weather is a bit colder. On the roof there is also a sort of gypsy-style van that is part of the decoration, which makes the space feel even more creative and fun.They offer a wide range of cocktails, as well as beers and other drinks, and there are also some nibbles available if you want something small to eat. Another feature that makes this place quite special is that you can book areas to play darts, which adds a social and interactive element to the experience.We only stopped by for a couple of drinks, a beer and a cocktail, but it was a very pleasant visit. Overall it’s definitely a place worth going to if you’re looking for somewhere lively and nicely designed to spend an evening or simply enjoy a few drinks with friends.

Fight Club in Cardiff is a fantastic spot right in the middle of the action. Surrounded by restaurants and bars, it really stands out with its unique character. The place has three levels, including a rooftop deck that offers a fun setting and a perfect place to hang out.The vibe is energetic but not too loud, and the clocks on the walls add an interesting touch to the decor. There’s plenty to do, whether you want to challenge friends to darts or just enjoy a drink.The food is genuinely good—definitely more than typical bar fare. With several bars throughout, you won’t have to wait long for your next round. Plus, the staff is friendly and attentive, making your visit feel welcoming.Located in a pedestrian zone, it’s a great stop after exploring the city. If you’re looking for a mix of good food, fun games, and a lively atmosphere, Fight Club is worth checking out.

Visit Summary:
We had an enjoyable visit overall. The highlight was definitely the game at the Oche—it was fun, engaging, and created a lively atmosphere. The food was also a real positive; the cheese and bacon waffle fries were a hit, and the nachos and waffle fries were tasty. Drinks were generally fine, but there were issues with the tab not working, which caused some delays and affected our experience slightly.
Service was polite, but staff attentiveness could be improved. We noticed limited checking throughout the visit, and there was no proactive guidance regarding allergies, which left me unsure if safe choices were being made. Some small details, like offering napkins, forks, or plates, also required us to ask at the bar. The team appeared professional in presentation, but they didn’t seem fully engaged, and their energy and enthusiasm could be higher.  Overall, the visit was positive, with great games, food, and atmosphere. More attentive service, consistent checking, and clearer allergy guidance would significantly enhance the guest experience.

Comments: I am writing to share feedback following our recent visit, which unfortunately left us disappointed with the level of service we received. This was the second time we have had a similar experience, so I felt it was important to raise our concerns.Firstly, we had pre-ordered pizzas and later requested to reduce the quantity by two. We were told this was not possible as the order had already been prepaid. At the time of the request, the pizzas had not yet been baked, and it seemed reasonable that the order could have been adjusted. The response felt inflexible and not particularly customer-focused.Secondly, I asked if we could be seated in a specific area that would allow two oches, as this would have better suited our group and the purpose of our visit, which was a team-building event. We were told these areas were reserved for a large group arriving later. However, throughout our entire stay, no such large group arrived, only a group of four people. Given that we were the larger group, it would have made far more sense to allow us to use this space, which would have significantly improved our experience. This was disappointing.As expected, we ended up with far too much pizza. When I asked for the remaining food to be packed up, I was given four sheets of foil and told that no more could be provided as foil “costs money.” I had to pop to tesco nearby to buy a pack of foil, for £1.65 to enable us to take some food with us. Considering the amount we spent during our visit, this felt extremely petty and unprofessional. The service also became rushed once the time reached 6:30pm, despite it being a quiet Monday evening. We were asked to leave the area in a manner that felt abrupt and unfriendly, leaving us feeling unwelcome rather than valued as customers. Overall, it felt as though customers who prepay are treated less favourably, which aligns with our previous experience (last year, where we spent £1,500+ ) and is why I felt compelled to speak up.We genuinely enjoy the concept of Flight Club, but service issues like these make it difficult to want to return.I hope you will take this feedback on board and review how similar situations are handled in the future.Its worth mentioning, when I was talking to your web support to understand how many oches we would be entitled to as a group of 13, the responses felt quite abrupt, ie- as I already said, ... it seems some of your colleagues need additional customer service training.
If someone would like to get back to me regarding this experience I am happy to talk about it.

Answer: I'm not sure what that is

Best decision of the night.Jess got us set up, explained the games without making us feel thick, and suddenly we were fiercely competitive over something we hadn’t cared about ten minutes earlier. Bullseyes were celebrated like cup finals. Misses were blamed on wind. Comments: Excellent staff who were very attentive

Visit Summary:
The initial welcome upon entering the venue was limited. No team member approached our table in the bar to offer a greeting or ask whether we were visiting for Social Darts or just food and drinks. 

However, when we approached the reception desk, a team member offered a warm “Hello, are you okay?” and asked if we were celebrating Mother’s Day. Both prosecco and alcohol-free prosecco were offered, which was a thoughtful touch. 

The team were polite, friendly, and engaging during interactions. Service, however, felt reactive rather than proactive. During gameplay, team members only attended the oche when the call button for food or drinks was pressed. There were no regular check-ins to offer drinks, check scores, or engage with us. Similarly, at the end of the booking, no team member approached the oche to advise that the session had finished or offer to move food and drinks, and no farewell was provided when leaving the venue. 

The team took allergies seriously, confidently guiding us through scanning the QR code to ensure dishes were safe to order. They clearly explained the steps and were able to answer questions about the menu confidently, demonstrating both knowledge and care. 

The venue was clean, tidy, and in excellent condition. Customer-facing areas, the oche, and bar were all well-maintained, creating a vibrant and energetic atmosphere that made the visit enjoyable. All food and drinks were of excellent taste, temperature, and presentation, representing excellent value for money, further enhancing the enjoyment of the visit.

Comments: All dishes were of excellent taste, presentation and were served at the correct temperature. The Peri Peri Chicken Burger was moist, succulent, and flavourful, with a distinct piri-piri taste that delivered a subtle kick of heat. It was served alongside fries that had a crispy exterior and soft centre, and homemade slaw that was crunchy with a fresh taste.All three pizzas were well-prepared, with toppings evenly distributed and dough evenly stretched, resulting in a light, crisp, and airy texture. The Tandoori Chicken pizza was visually appealing, with the vibrant colours of red onion, coriander, and golden poppadoms contrasting nicely against the melted cheese and tomato base. The mint yoghurt added a cooling creaminess, balancing the warmth and spices of the tandoori chicken, while the mango chutney contributed a subtle sweetness that complemented the savoury toppings. The crispy poppadoms added a textural contrast, giving a satisfying crunch alongside the soft, airy base. The Nduja Heatwave had a bold appearance, with the deep red of the roquito peppers and vibrant green rocket standing out against the tomato sauce. The Nduja offered a rich, spicy kick, while the hot honey drizzle added a sweet heat. The rocket contributed a fresh, peppery note that cut through the richness of the Nduja. The Veggie Feast pizza was colourful and fresh, with roasted red peppers, red onion, and portobello mushrooms arranged evenly across the base. The tomato sauce was rich and well-seasoned, complementing the creamy mozzarella and roasted vegetables. The red pepper sauce and herb oil added layers of flavour that enhanced the overall freshness.

After six or seven minutes, nobody came to take our food and drink order, so I ordered this at the bar, which I assumed was the process for this venue. Once the food arrived, the waitress checked in on us, but it would've been nice to be thanked and given a departing comment when we left the venue.

Comments: it was my birthday, I had the best time at the flight club. The staff was so good, they deserve the hike, they were all absolutely friendly, and helpful.
There was one Indian guy who served us and also 1 more person apart from him. they were all absolutely lovely. It was my birthday, had the best time.

Visit Summary: Overall, my visit to the Flight Club was a very positive social experience that provided a great opportunity for me to spend quality time with my fellow guests. The service of all the staff was very good, they were attentive throughout, ensuring we were happy and always had everything we needed. There was a good range of food and drink, all of which were very high quality. We very much enjoyed all the food we had. It felt fresh and had a good flavour. The atmosphere in the venue was very relaxed, the music playing was great. Some small comments for improvement could be that there was quite a delay between our mains with allergens and not coming out. This delay was over 5 minutes. This meant half of our party were eating whilst the other half were still waiting for their food. If these dishes were delivered to the table closer together, that would have been more appropriate. Also, I believe the welcome could have been slightly warmer, by making more of an effort for it to not feel so transactional. However, this was the only part of the process that felt this was, so it is only a small improvement to be made.

Comments: All the drinks we were served were to a very high standard. They all had great flavour, especially the cocktail, which was very sweet and just as described. All drinks came well chilled and in clean glasses as expected.
